# clawleash

> clawleash is an open-source CLI that lets you approve or deny Claude Code
> permission prompts from your phone, so long autonomous runs never stall while
> you're away from the desk. It is self-hosted and token-gated, reaching your
> Mac over Tailscale or the same Wi-Fi — no cloud relay, no account.

## What it does

- Approve/deny Claude Code permission prompts (Bash, Write, Edit, …) from a phone web page.
- See live agent status (which sessions are running, which subagents).
- Optional push notifications via ntfy when a prompt needs you.

## How it works

- Installs Claude Code hooks into `~/.claude/settings.json` with one command.
- A tiny local Node server holds the `PermissionRequest` hook open until you
  answer on your phone (or it times out and falls back to the terminal prompt).
- The phone opens a token-gated, installable PWA served on your machine; it
  reaches the Mac over Tailscale (anywhere) or the same Wi-Fi (at home).

## Quick start

```
npx clawleash
```
Then scan the printed URL on your phone and Add to Home Screen.

## Key facts

- License: Apache-2.0. Self-hosted. No cloud, no subscription required.
- Security: off-by-default remote approvals, secret token (`?k=`), 403 without it,
  hook ingress is loopback-only, headless sessions auto-deny, no-response falls
  back to the desktop terminal prompt.
- Not affiliated with Anthropic; a community companion for Claude Code.

## Links

- Repo: https://github.com/wilsonwang0713/clawleash
- README: https://github.com/wilsonwang0713/clawleash#readme
